steps.title: Compute the boundary values
steps.content:
For the inequality $y>4x+8$, compute the right‑hand side at the given x‑values.
$$4(2)+8 = 16$$
$$4(4)+8 = 24$$
$$4(6)+8 = 32$$
Thus a pair $(x,y)$ satisfies the inequality exactly when $y>16$ for $x=2$, $y>24$ for $x=4$, and $y>32$ for $x=6$.

steps.title: Test each table against the boundary
steps.content:
Check each (x,y) pair.
Choice A:
(2,19): \$19>16$ ✓
(4,30): \$30>24$ ✓
(6,41): \$41>32$ ✓
All three satisfy.
Choice B:
(2,8): \$8>16$ ✗ → fails.
Choice C:
(2,13): \$13>16$ ✗ → fails.
Choice D:
(2,13): \$13>16$ ✗ → fails.
Only Choice A has every pair above the threshold.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
